_doc: Declarative DB-schema document. The generator at server/cmd/schema-gen reads this file and emits CREATE TABLE / CREATE INDEX statements. A parity test in server/internal/schema/declarative ensures the generated schema matches what the migrations produce, table-for-table. Scope is intentionally limited in v1 to a handful of simple tables — role, role_mapping, comment_body, process, process_step — until the document grows enough coverage to displace the migration chain. See task #18.

tables.name: role
tables.doc: Application role; user_role assigns these to user_account rows, role_grant lists what (card_type, process) pairs each role may invoke. Doc column was added in migration 0010.

tables.name: process
tables.doc: Logical workflow name (e.g. card.create). process_step rows declare the ordered handler chain.

tables.name: process_step
tables.doc: One step of a process — a (endpoint, action) tuple targeted in ordinal order.

tables.name: comment_body
tables.doc: Comment text attached to a card via an activity row of kind=comment.

tables.name: role_mapping
tables.doc: OIDC group claim → role mapping. Added in migration 0010.
